name: husky (Git Hooks for Node.js Projects)

performance.notes: Hook execution time depends on what's run (lint, tests, etc.)

agent_readiness.notes: Git hooks manager for Node.js. npm install --save-dev husky. npx husky init → creates .husky/ and adds prepare script. .husky/pre-commit: #!/usr/bin/env sh; . '$(dirname -- '$0')/_/husky.sh'; npx lint-staged. .husky/commit-msg: npx --no -- commitlint --edit $1. .husky/pre-push: npm test. v9 uses .husky/ files directly — no JSON config. husky uses git core.hooksPath. Disable per-commit: HUSKY=0 git commit. Often combined with lint-staged (run linters only on staged files) and commitlint (validate commit messages). CI: HUSKY=0 set in CI to skip hooks. Shell script per hook in .husky/ directory.

gotchas: v9 breaking change from v8 — .husky/_ directory structure changed
Windows requires Git for Windows with bash
HUSKY=0 needed in CI to skip hooks
Hooks can be bypassed with git commit --no-verify
Not installed automatically for cloners unless prepare script runs
best_when: Enforcing pre-commit linting, formatting, or tests in Node.js projects
Combined with lint-staged for staged-file-only checks
Team projects ensuring code quality gates before commits
avoid_when: CI/CD (use CI checks instead of hooks)
Non-Node.js projects (use lefthook or pre-commit instead)
summary: Husky manages Git hooks for Node.js. npx husky init; .husky/pre-commit with lint-staged; .husky/commit-msg with commitlint. Shell scripts per hook in .husky/. v9 uses file-based hooks (no JSON config). MIT by typicode. HUSKY=0 for CI skip. Often paired with lint-staged and commitlint. Non-Node projects should use lefthook or pre-commit. Bypassed with --no-verify.
